blocks.content: Every ADK agent is defined by a primary metadata and configuration file named `agent.yaml`. This file explicitly declares the agent's identity, the execution model, model hyperparameters, registered tool modules, and conversation memory windows.
Below is a complete, production-grade example of an `agent.yaml` definition file:

blocks.code:
version: "1.0.0"
agent_id: "document-analyzer-agent"
title: "Enterprise Document Analyzer"
description: "Extracts structured schema facts from uploaded financial reports."

model:
  provider: "google-vertex"
  name: "gemini-1.5-pro"
  parameters:
    temperature: 0.1
    top_p: 0.95
    max_output_tokens: 8192

memory:
  type: "sliding-window"
  window_size: 15
  compaction_policy: "summarize"

tools:
  - name: "read_file_content"
    source: "src/tools/file_ops.py"
    class: "ReadFileContentTool"
  - name: "regex_search"
    source: "src/tools/text_regex.py"
    class: "RegexSearchTool"

security:
  workspace_lock: true
  network_access: "restricted"
  allowed_domains:
    - "*.googleapis.com"

items: model.parameters.temperature: Determines token sampling randomness. In engineering tools, keep this low (0.0 to 0.2) to guarantee deterministic code outputs and strict schema adherence.

memory.compaction_policy: Defines how the agent compresses messages when context thresholds are exceeded. 'summarize' triggers a background call to summarize older messages, while 'truncate' deletes oldest turns.
security.workspace_lock: When set to true, restricts the agent from reading or writing files outside of its immediate execution folder.

blocks.content: Question 1: Why should an agent.yaml used for backend data migration have a temperature parameter of 0.0 or 0.1?

- A) To reduce memory allocation size in the container.
- B) To ensure consistent, deterministic behavior and minimize hallucinated variables during parsing.
- C) To make the agent run twice as fast.
Feedback: Option B is correct. Lowering the temperature decreases model randomness, which is critical for structured data processing and code execution.
